embeds.title: Lineages
embeds.description:
*Please note that for all characters created using a Lineage with the `Ancestral Legacy` feature, that feature only offers the two skill proficiencies.*
> __**Ancestral Legacy**__
> If you replace a race with this lineage, you can keep the following elements of that race: any skill proficiencies you gained from it and any climbing, flying, or swimming speed you gained from it.
> If you don’t keep any of those elements **or you choose this lineage at character creation**, you gain proficiency in two skills of your choice.

embeds.title: Custom Origin
embeds.description:
Tasha's Cauldron of Everything introduced an optional rule to customize aspects of your character. The default ability score increases / languages / and proficiencies for each race reflects the typical members of that race, but not all individuals are the same. You may customize your character in the following ways:
**If you choose any of these options, you must specify the changes when presenting your character for approval.**

fields.name: Ability Score Increases
fields.value:
You may choose to ignore your standard racial ASI and assign ability score increases tailored to your character by taking any ability score increase you gain in your race or subrace and apply it to an ability score of your choice.
 • If you gain more than one increase, you can’t apply those increases to the same ability score
 • You can’t increase a score above 20.

*This is built into Beyond, but [requires customizing the bonuses in GSheet](https://discord.com/channels/694275190976413816/775178179941892096/785958769938268198).*

fields.name: Languages
fields.value: You may replace any language granted to you with another language from the following list: Abyssal, Celestial, Common, Deep Speech, Draconic, Dwarvish, Elvish, Giant, Gnomish, Goblin, Halfling, Infernal, Orc, Primordial, Sylvan, or Undercommon.

fields.name: Proficiencies
fields.value: You can replace any proficiency granted by your race with a different one of your choice, following the table below.

fields.name: Granted Proficiency
fields.value:
Armor
Martial Weapon
Simple Weapon
Tool

fields.name: Replacement Proficiency Options
fields.value:
Simple/Martial Weapon or Tool
Simple/Martial Weapon or Tool
Simple Weapon or Tool
Simple Weapon or Tool

fields.name: Changing a Skill
fields.value: Sometimes you pick a skill proficiency that ends up not being very useful in the campaign or that no longer fits your character’s story. In those cases, talk to your DM about replacing that skill proficiency with another skill proficiency offered by your class at 1st level. A convenient time for such a change is when you reach a level that grants you the Ability Score Increase feature, representing that your character has spent a level or two studying the new skill and letting the old one atrophy.

fields.name: Changing Your Subclass
fields.value:
Each character class involves the choice of a subclass at 1st, 2nd, or 3rd level. A subclass represents an area of specialization and offers different class features as you level up. You can change your subclass only when you gain a level that would normally give a new subclass feature.
If you decide to make this change, choose another subclass that belongs to your class and replace all your old subclass features with the features of the new subclass that are for your new level and lower.

fields.name: Changing Your Spells
fields.value:
In order to allow players the flexibility to choose spells for the purposes of (e)RP, while not locking them into potentially sub-optimal choices for the purposes of duels and tables, we allow the UA Spell Versatility feature as described in [this PDF](https://media.wizards.com/2019/dnd/downloads/UA-ClassFeatures.pdf):
In short, this feature allows non-preparation casters (Bards / Rangers / Sorcerers / Warlocks) to swap spells once per long rest, as follows:
```Whenever you finish a long rest, you can replace one spell you learned from your Spellcasting feature with another spell from your spell list. The new spell must be the same level as the spell you replace.```
